findings.evidence: voice.service.ts:197 — `logger.info(\`[Voice] Transcribed: "${cleanText}"\`)` logs the full spoken command at info level. memory.service.ts:188 — `logger.info(\`Memory forcefully updated with Manual Override for goal: ${goal}\`)` logs the raw goal text at info level.
findings.fix: Replace verbatim content with length/redacted indicators at info level: `logger.info(\`[Voice] Transcribed (${cleanText.length} chars)\`)`. Gate full text behind `logger.debug(...)`. Same for the goal string in memory.service.ts injectOverride.

findings.subsystem: Remaining src services + utils (memory, session, ocr, voice, metrics, backgroundDaemon, readiness, uiParser, presets, hardware, logger, memory util, notes, stripControlTokens)
findings.title: stripControlTokens misses several modern chat templates (Gemma, Mistral [INST], Phi-3, raw end_header) — control-token smuggling
findings.evidence: stripControlTokens.ts:7-23 — CONTROL_TOKEN_PATTERNS has 8 entries covering ChatML, Llama/Phi end-of-turn, legacy EOS, tool_call XML, and Llama 3 header pairs. Missing: Gemma `<start_of_turn>`/`<end_of_turn>(\w*)`, Mistral `[INST]`/`[/INST]`, `<<SYS>>`/`<</SYS>>`, Phi-3 `<|user|>`/`<|assistant|>`/`<|system|>`, and standalone `<|end_header_id|>` / `<|start_header_id|>`.
findings.fix: Add to CONTROL_TOKEN_PATTERNS: `/<start_of_turn>\w*\n?/gi`, `/<end_of_turn>/gi`, `/\[\/?INST\]/gi`, `/<<\/?SYS>>/gi`, `/<\|(user|assistant|system)\|>/gi`, and standalone `/<\|end_header_id\|>/gi`.

findings.subsystem: Remaining src services + utils (memory, session, ocr, voice, metrics, backgroundDaemon, readiness, uiParser, presets, hardware, logger, memory util, notes, stripControlTokens)
findings.title: Daemon habit log unbounded-growth + non-atomic compaction race
findings.evidence: metrics.service.ts:94-98 — compaction is triggered inside `getHabits()` (a reader), fires `fs.writeFile(...).catch(() => {})` with no lock. A concurrent `appendHabitLog` append can interleave with this rewrite, silently losing records. No write mutex is present in MetricsService.
findings.fix: Move compaction into `appendHabitLog` (the writer) via a size/line-count check after append, and serialize both the append and the compaction rewrite through a promise-chain mutex (as MemoryService does), or write-to-temp-then-rename for atomicity.

findings.subsystem: Remaining src services + utils (memory, session, ocr, voice, metrics, backgroundDaemon, readiness, uiParser, presets, hardware, logger, memory util, notes, stripControlTokens)
findings.title: Concurrent metrics writers can drop records (read-modify-write with no lock)
findings.evidence: metrics.service.ts:48-60 — `appendMetrics` does `readFile → JSON.parse → records.push → writeFile` with no mutex. Two concurrent callers both read the same snapshot, each push their record, and the second write clobbers the first, dropping the earlier metric.
findings.fix: Add a promise-chain write mutex (e.g. `this._writeLock = this._writeLock.then(() => doAppend())`) matching the pattern in MemoryService, or migrate telemetry to NDJSON append (`fs.appendFile`) like `habitsNdjsonPath` already uses.

findings.subsystem: Remaining src services + utils (memory, session, ocr, voice, metrics, backgroundDaemon, readiness, uiParser, presets, hardware, logger, memory util, notes, stripControlTokens)
findings.title: Background daemon poll loop has no shutdown/lifecycle teardown; restart spawns a second loop
findings.evidence: backgroundDaemon.service.ts:41-51,116-184 — `stop()` sets `this.isRunning = false` but returns void immediately; the poll loop's `while (this.isRunning)` only checks the flag at the top of each iteration, after the current `setTimeout` resolves (up to pollIntervalMs later). No AbortController threads into deviceProvider/llmProvider calls; no promise that callers can await to confirm the loop has actually exited.
findings.fix: Add a `_stopPromise: Promise<void>` and resolver; resolve it when the while-loop exits. Have `stop()` return that promise so callers can `await daemon.stop()`. Optionally thread an AbortSignal into long-running provider calls so they abort immediately on stop.
